root_cause: Sustained load factor above 1.0 pu combined with depressed secondary voltage (≤ 0.95 pu) suggests downstream demand has outgrown the transformer's nameplate rating, or load distribution across phases has become uneven. Sustained over-loading consumes accelerated insulation life even when oil temperature stays nominal.

recommended_actions.action: Re-route a portion of the downstream feeder load to an adjacent transformer if grid topology permits, or coordinate with operations to shed non-critical load during peak windows.
recommended_actions.rationale: Bringing the load factor below 1.0 is the cheapest way to halt accelerated aging. Topology re-routing is reversible; insulation damage is not.

recommended_actions.action: Verify per-phase current balance via SCADA or on-site clamp meter.
recommended_actions.rationale: Imbalance > 10% between phases indicates a downstream fault or single-phase load growth and changes the recommended remediation.

recommended_actions.action: Add this asset to the load-growth review for the next capital plan cycle.
recommended_actions.rationale: If load continues to grow at observed rate, the asset warrants a capacity upgrade or splitting of the downstream feeder.
